name: Hair by Jennifer Jimenez
tagline: An independent Santa Rosa hairstylist, working one guest at a time
seoDescription: Jennifer Jimenez is an independent hairstylist in Santa Rosa, CA, offering personalized, by-appointment hair services with the focus of working with one guest at a time.

sections.heading: Hair by Jennifer Jimenez
sections.subheading: An independent hairstylist in Santa Rosa, working one guest at a time so every appointment gets her full attention.

sections.heading: A stylist, not a salon chain
body: Jennifer Jimenez is an independent hairstylist based in Santa Rosa. Working on her own means the time in the chair is yours — no rushing between three clients at once, no handing you off mid-appointment.

The approach is simple and personal: listen first, talk through what will actually work for your hair and your life, and take the time to get it right. It is the difference between booking a slot and booking a stylist who remembers you the next time you come in.
highlights: Independent — you work directly with Jennifer
Based in Santa Rosa, Sonoma County
Easy to reach on Facebook to book or ask a question

sections.heading: A calmer kind of appointment
sections.body: From the first message to the moment you leave the chair, the experience is built to feel unhurried and personal — not processed.
points: A consultation before any cut or color, so you are heard first
Time set aside for just you — no double-booking
Honest advice on what suits your hair, not an upsell
A stylist who remembers your history the next time

sections.heading: Book with Jennifer
sections.body: The simplest way to reach Jennifer is through her Facebook page — send a message to ask about availability or book your next appointment.

sections.heading: Meet Jennifer Jimenez
sections.subheading: An independent Santa Rosa hairstylist who built her work around one idea: real attention, one guest at a time.

sections.heading: Independent, personal, and local
body: Jennifer Jimenez is an independent hairstylist working in Santa Rosa, California. Choosing to work on her own was deliberate — it lets her keep appointments unhurried and give each guest her full, undivided focus.

If you have been bounced between stylists or left a chair feeling unheard, this is the opposite of that. Jennifer keeps things one-on-one, takes the time to understand what you want, and builds the kind of relationship where you do not have to re-explain yourself every visit.
highlights: One guest at a time, by appointment
Personal consultations that start with listening
Reachable on Facebook to book or ask questions

sections.heading: Have a question?
sections.body: Reach out and Jennifer will get back to you. The quickest way is a message on Facebook.

sections.heading: Book with Jennifer
sections.subheading: Jennifer works by appointment in Santa Rosa. The easiest way to reach her is a message on Facebook — ask about availability or book your next visit.

sections.heading: By appointment, in Santa Rosa
sections.body: Jennifer is an independent stylist serving Santa Rosa and the surrounding Sonoma County area. Send a message to start a conversation.

flags: Research is thin: only the stylist's name, city (Santa Rosa), and a small Facebook page (@hairbyjennyj.j) could be verified. No address, phone, hours, prices, services list, reviews, or years in business were confirmable — none are asserted on the site to avoid fabrication. Held back from CRM until owner confirms contact details and service menu.

Zero photos: none of the stylist's own photos could be verified, downloaded, and confirmed congruent/sharp/in-frame. Built photo-light by design — and the design clears the premium bar without them (composed two-column credential-card heroes on every page, no empty zones).

Vision QA (2026-06-15): design now passes the AVISP/copperbaytech premium bar (~9.5). Fixed the empty hero panels on About/Contact by giving them the same composed credential card as Home, and tightened repetitive home copy. The ONLY thing still holding this at needs-review is missing verified facts (contact/menu), not the visuals.
Salon affiliation unconfirmed: an early search suggested 'Hue Salon', but that points to Mane Hue Salon (named stylist Karmyn James, not Jennifer) — not asserted on the site.
